skill.name: qryma-search
skill.description: Search the web with multiple output formats using the Qryma API. Use this skill when the user wants to search the web, find information on a specific topic, says "search", "look up", "find information", "web search", or needs quick answers from the internet. Supports Markdown format for readability, JSON for structured data, and Brave search-like format.
skill.summary: Web search skill with Markdown, JSON, and Brave-style output formats powered by the Qryma search API.
skill.author: qryma-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 112 patterns but evaluation shows these are false positives. Environment variable access (QRYMA_API_KEY, QRYMA_ENDPOINT) is standard configuration for API-based tools. Network requests to search.qryma.com represent core functionality. Hidden file access (~/.qryma/.env) is legitimate config storage. No malicious patterns, credential exfiltration, or obfuscation detected. The skill is a transparent web search tool requiring user-provided API credentials.

medium_findings.title: Configuration File in Home Directory
medium_findings.description: The skill reads configuration from ~/.qryma/.env which is a hidden directory in the user's home folder. While this is standard practice for CLI tools, it represents elevated filesystem access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern confirms hidden file access but purpose is legitimate configuration storage, not data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Configuration
low_findings.description: The skill reads QRYMA_API_KEY and QRYMA_ENDPOINT from environment variables. This is standard practice but represents access to sensitive configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed environment variable access for API credentials - expected behavior for API-based tools.

low_findings.title: HTTPS Network Requests to External API
low_findings.description: The skill makes POST requests to https://search.qryma.com/api/web to perform searches. This is core functionality but represents network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed network requests to documented API endpoint - legitimate search functionality.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API Endpoint URL
low_findings.description: Default endpoint URL is hardcoded in the source code. This is acceptable for a skill designed to work with a specific service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Hardcoded URL to official Qryma service - expected for vendor-specific integration.

content.user_title: Search the Web with Qryma API
content.value_statement: Quickly find information online using the Qryma search engine. Get results in Markdown, JSON, or Brave-style formats optimized for AI assistants and developers.
actual_capabilities: Execute web searches using the Qryma search engine API
Return results in Markdown format for readable output
Return results in JSON format for programmatic processing
Return results in Brave search-style format
Support multiple languages and safe search options
Paginate through results with start offset parameter
limitations: Requires a valid Qryma API key to function
Limited to search functionality only - no browsing or page content extraction
Results depend on Qryma API availability and rate limits
Cannot access paid or subscription-only search features

use_cases.title: Research Assistant
use_cases.description: Quickly gather information on topics for research projects, homework, or personal learning.

use_cases.title: Developer Documentation Search
use_cases.description: Find programming solutions, API documentation, and technical answers during development.

use_cases.title: Fact-Checking and Verification
use_cases.description: Verify claims, find sources, and gather evidence for content creation or decision-making.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for information about [topic] and show me the top 5 results in Markdown format.

prompt_templates.title: Structured Data Export
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for [query] and return the results as JSON so I can process them programmatically.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Language Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for [query] in [language code, e.g., es, fr, de] and return up to 10 results.

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Research Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a detailed search for [complex topic] with safe search enabled, returning comprehensive results in Markdown format with full snippets.

output_examples.input: Search for 'how to learn Python programming' with 3 results in Markdown
output_examples.output:
1. Learn Python - Free Interactive Python Tutorial
   https://www.learnpython.org
   - Free interactive Python tutorial for beginners with hands-on exercises

2. Python.org - Getting Started
   https://www.python.org/about/gettingstarted/
   - Official Python documentation and beginner resources

3. Codecademy - Learn Python 3
   https://www.codecademy.com/learn/learn-python-3
   - Interactive Python course with real-world projects

output_examples.input: Search for 'latest AI trends 2024' in JSON format
output_examples.output: Search results returned as structured JSON with query, results array containing title, url, and content fields for each result

best_practices: Always provide specific, focused search queries for better results
Use the Markdown format for human-readable output and JSON for programmatic use
Start with fewer results (3-5) and increase only if needed to reduce API usage
anti_patterns: Do not use for sensitive queries that should not be sent to external APIs
Avoid excessive polling or automated bulk searches that may exceed rate limits
Do not rely on search results for critical decisions without verification

faq.question: How do I get a Qryma API key?
faq.answer: Visit the Qryma website and sign up for an account. They offer a free tier with generous limits for personal use.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without an API key?
faq.answer: No, a valid Qryma API key is required. The skill will return an error if the key is missing or invalid.

faq.question: What output format should I use?
faq.answer: Use Markdown (md) for reading results, JSON (raw) for programmatic processing, or Brave format for a familiar search engine layout.

faq.question: Is my search query sent to external servers?
faq.answer: Yes, your query is sent to the Qryma API at search.qryma.com. Do not search for sensitive or private information.

faq.question: How many results can I retrieve at once?
faq.answer: The default is 5 results, but you can request more using the --max-results parameter. Higher values may use more API quota.

faq.question: Can I search in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --lang parameter with ISO language codes like 'es' for Spanish, 'fr' for French, or 'de' for German.
